steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank begins the second sentence, which explains what NASA did in response to Curiosity's limitation. The first sentence establishes the problem: Curiosity couldn't preserve samples. The second sentence says NASA sought to fix that. The blank must signal a consequence — something that follows logically from the problem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Previously,': signals that something happened before — but designing Perseverance happened after Curiosity's limitation was known, not before. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Specifically,': signals a narrowing or clarification of the previous statement — but the second sentence introduces a new action (building Perseverance), not a detail of Curiosity's limitation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Likewise,': signals similarity — but Perseverance was built to do something Curiosity could not, so the two are contrasted, not compared. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Thus,': signals a consequence — because Curiosity couldn't preserve samples, NASA created Perseverance to do exactly that. This directly connects cause and effect. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
